programmes.show_description: A film from long-time Pink Floyd collaborator Ian Emes, offering a captivating and intuitive interpretation of the Rock'n'Roll legends' music.
The line-up changed, the style evolved, but the hits kept coming. From Peter Gabriel to Phil Collins, Invisible Touch to I Can't Dance, a documentary that charts the inspiration of Genesis.
The Joshua Tree: Bono, The Edge, Adam Clayton, Larry Mullen and producers Brian Eno and Daniel Lanois give an insight into the making of their 1987 smash The Joshua Tree. (S1, ep 18)
Aya Eban: Created by NikNak, Randy Duburke and Peta-Gaye Brown, follow a character stuck in a hectic city without peace - until their ancestors help them discover their power. (2 of 3)
Accompanied by the Johann Strauss Orchestra, world renowned violinist Andre Rieu performs numbers including Roses from the South, and Emperor's Waltz at Schonbrunn in Vienna.
The Way Up To Heaven: Mrs Foster exacts an intriguing revenge on her husband after his dawdling causes her to miss a flight. (S1, ep 9)
Depart in Peace: Art dealer Lionel gets an artist to visualise his fiancee Janet nude and paint her accordingly. Janet, however, does not see the funny side. (S1, ep 10)
My Ladylove, My Dove: When Pamela and Arthur invite a young couple over for the weekend, things take a surprising turn. (S1, ep 11)
Taste: A dinner party takes a surprising turn when one pretentious guest is challenged to a wine-tasting contest. (S1, ep 12)
Behind the Locked Door: A young couple tries to convince the bride's mother that their love is sincere. (S2, ep 22)
Best Of: Stephen Mangan presents this special episode featuring highlights from the latest series of Landscape Artist of the Year. (S11, ep 10)
Sky Original documentary tracing the complex history of Michelangelo's most controversial commission, the Mausoleum of Pope Julius II, which took 40 years to complete.
A look at the life and career of two-time Academy Award-winning British film icon Sir Michael Caine. (S10, ep 4)
...Remembering The Carpenters. Exploration of the highs and lows of the lives and careers of the brother-sister duo, including Karen's untimely death in 1983. With Burt Bacharach.

programmes.show_name: Luther Vandross: Live At The Royal Albert Hall
programmes.show_description: Enjoy this glitzy, upbeat celebration of the greatest music from Luther Vandross, featuring a full gospel choir.

programmes.show_name: Rod Stewart: It Had To Be You
programmes.show_description: Filmed in front of a live studio audience at Sony Studios in New York City, Rod Stewart performs songs from his recent release "It Had to be You: The Great American Songbook".
A historical drama film about the founding of the State of Israel. Contains outdated attitudes and cultural depictions which viewers may find inconsistent with contemporary values.
Celebrate Opry 100 with superstars, newcomers and legends - it's never the same show twice! Jon Pardi, Zach Top and Ashley McBryde.
Full coverage of the iconic Isle of Wight festival, one of the UK's biggest and most legendary highlights of the summer. Featuring Texas.
...Live At Wembley Stadium 2000. Tina Turner takes the stage at Wembley Stadium as the Queen of Rock'n'Roll puts on an electrifying performance as part of her Twenty Four Seven tour.
A fascinating look at the making of John Lennon's debut solo album, Plastic Ono Band, which saw the former Beatle dealing with issues such as abandonment, class, love and God.
A grand Italian-themed procession and festive parade imagined by a clown with awesome acrobatics, as the world-famous human circus explores a mysterious place between heaven and earth.
Art competition in which amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours. (S2, ep 5)
Art competition in which amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours. (S2, ep 6)

Depart in Peace: Art dealer Lionel gets an artist to visualise his fiancee Janet nude and paint her accordingly. Janet, however, does not see the funny side. (S1, ep 10)
Gentleman From America: Rich American Howard Latimer takes a trip to London, betting £1,000 he can stay in a room that is said to be haunted. (S1, ep 31)
The Baby Sitter: When Clara Nash is murdered, her babysitter Lottie Slocum is excited to be the centre of attention, and she also hopes to become closer to Clara's husband. (S1, ep 32)
A look at the life and career of the Academy Award-winning director, whose films include The Godfather franchise and Apocalypse Now. (S5, ep 1)
A glimpse into the life and hugely successful career of Academy Award-winning actress and activist, Susan Sarandon. (S10, ep 6)

Kate journeys through the gallery's 200-year history with a look at the Turner bequest, before exploring the impressionist works by Van Gogh, Monet and Seurat. (2 of 2)

A look at the life and career of the Academy Award-winning director, whose films include Alien, Blade Runner, Thelma & Louise, Gladiator and Hannibal. (S5, ep 2)
A look into the life and legacy of Academy Award-winning actor Christopher Plummer, who has sadly passed away. (S10, ep 7)
Galloping Foxley: John Mills stars in this twisted tale. A man starts to suffer when he becomes haunted by the memory of the brutal treatment he received in his childhood. (S2, ep 2)
Georgy Porgy: Joan Collins stars in a twisted tale. George has problems with female attention, but when a beautiful woman joins his congregation things are set to change. (S2, ep 3)
The Belfry: When Clint Ringle learns that Ellie Marsh, the schoolteacher he loves, is engaged to Walt Norton, Clint kills Walt and then hides in the school's bell tower. (S1, ep 33)
The Hidden Thing: After Dana Edwards' fiancée Laura is killed in a hit-and-run, he is visited by John Hurley, who claims to be able to help him to remember the car's plate. (S1, ep 34)
Violin sensation Andre Rieu and his Johann Strauss Orchestra take to the stage in the Irish capital for a lively and enchanting performance of his best-loved music.
Enjoy a star-studded event at the Verona Arena honouring the life of tenor Luciano Pavarotti. With touching performances from Placido Domingo, Jose Carreras and many others.
Barry Cryer reflects on the life and career of multi-Emmy Award-winning comedian and actress Lily Tomlin. (S2, ep 2)
